title: Laravel Application Bootstrap & Service Registration
project_context: Datadog PHP tracing SDK — Laravel test fixtures used for integration/overhead testing across multiple Laravel versions (5.7, 5.8, 8.x, 9.x, 10.x, 11.x).
boot_sequence_phases: PHASE 1 — Load Provider List: Read config/app.php 'providers' array (10.x) OR bootstrap/providers.php (11.x)
PHASE 2 — Instantiate Providers: Kernel instantiates each ServiceProvider with app container
PHASE 3 — register() Pass: Call register() on every provider before any boot() (binds services into IoC container)
PHASE 4 — boot() Pass: Call boot() on every provider in registration order after all register() complete
activity_flow: START: HTTP request or artisan command triggers application bootstrap
Load provider list from config/app.php OR bootstrap/providers.php
[10.x] ServiceProvider::defaultProviders()->merge([AppServiceProvider, AuthServiceProvider, BroadcastServiceProvider*, EventServiceProvider, RouteServiceProvider])
[11.x] Only AppServiceProvider in bootstrap/providers.php — others removed/consolidated
--- REGISTER PASS ---
AppServiceProvider::register(): void — empty (no IoC bindings in test versions)
AuthServiceProvider::register(): void — inherited; auto-registers policies from $policies[]
BroadcastServiceProvider — no register() override
EventServiceProvider::register(): void — inherited; auto-registers $listen[] event-listener mappings
--- BOOT PASS ---
AppServiceProvider::boot(): void — Schema::defaultStringLength(191) [sets DB varchar default to 191 for utf8mb4 compatibility]
AuthServiceProvider::boot(): void — empty (no Gate::define() calls in test fixture)
[condition] BroadcastServiceProvider enabled? (commented out in 10.x config by default)
YES → BroadcastServiceProvider::boot(): void — Broadcast::routes() + require base_path('routes/channels.php')
NO → Skip BroadcastServiceProvider boot
EventServiceProvider::boot(): void — empty override
EventServiceProvider::shouldDiscoverEvents(): bool — returns false (auto-discovery disabled)
[condition] shouldDiscoverEvents() == true?
YES → Auto-discover event listeners in Listeners/ directory
NO → Use only manually declared $listen[] mappings
$listen map: Registered::class => [SendEmailVerificationNotification::class]
END: All providers booted, application ready to handle requests

boot_actions: no-op (empty boot, empty policies in test fixture)
provider_details.note: Gate::define() calls would be added here for authorization rules
Commented out by default in config/app.php (10.x); disabled unless explicitly enabled

key_conditions: BroadcastServiceProvider enabled? — controlled by commenting/uncommenting in config/app.php
shouldDiscoverEvents() returns false → event listeners must be manually declared in $listen
Laravel 11.x consolidates providers: only AppServiceProvider kept; Auth/Broadcast/Event merged into core or AppServiceProvider
key_relationships: All providers registered in IoC Container via Kernel

version_differences.10.x_and_below: 4 providers in config/app.php; BroadcastServiceProvider commented out by default; Schema::defaultStringLength(191) in AppServiceProvider::boot()
version_differences.11.x: Single AppServiceProvider in bootstrap/providers.php; Auth/Event/Broadcast functionality moved to core or merged; AppServiceProvider::boot() empty

important_notes: This is a Datadog PHP tracing SDK project. The Laravel service providers are TEST FIXTURES for integration testing across framework versions — not production app logic. The bootstrap sequence is standard Laravel; the SDK instruments it via hooks/extensions to trace service provider registration timing.
